_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
reference_document_element_description: Define and enforce model development standards, including documentation requirements, testing protocols, approval gates, ongoing monitoring expectations, and the criteria for model retirement or replacement.
comments: NICE task intersects with WIDAI RM domain. Task RM-T-008 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-001 (RC, cosine: 0.568), OP-T-010 (OP, cosine: 0.525).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: STS 6.4/10 (raw 0.643). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. NICE-only terms [develop, guidelines, implementation] vs WIDAI-only terms [approval, criteria, define, development, documentation, enforce] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.874 (P=0.903, R=0.847). P exceeds R by 0.056 — the NICE element's 3 words are well-captured by the WIDAI KSA's 24 words, but the KSA contains additional specificity not reflected in the NICE element.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.1%). "Develop implementation guidelines" and "Define and enforce model development standards, including documentation requirem" are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.576 < STS 0.643 (gap -0.068). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [develop, guidelines, implementation] and [approval, criteria, define] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 0.0%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. NICE terms: [develop, guidelines, implementation]. WIDAI terms: [approval, criteria, define, development, documentation, enforce].
